formulas.name: Biodiversity Hotspots Law
formulas.notes: Regions with high levels of endemism and high levels of threat to those endemics.

formulas.name: Loss of Biodiversity
formulas.notes: Loss of biodiversity is a result of habitat loss and fragmentation, pollution, overexploitation, climate change and invasive species.

formulas.name: Endemic Species
formulas.notes: Species that are found only in a particular region or area.

formulas.name: Biodiversity Conservation
formulas.notes: Biodiversity conservation is the practice of preserving genetic diversity, species diversity, and ecosystem diversity to maintain ecosystem services and promote sustainable development.

formulas.name: Red Data Book
formulas.notes: It is a publication that lists threatened species in the world.

formulas.name: Protected Areas
formulas.notes: Protected areas are designated land or sea areas that are set aside to protect and conserve biodiversity.

formulas.name: Ecosystem Services
formulas.notes: Ecosystem services are the benefits that people obtain from functioning ecosystems, including air and water filtration, soil formation, and climate regulation.
